{\\n517: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"can't find NodeResourcesFitArgs in plugin config\\\")\\n518: \\t\\t}\\n519: \\t}\\n520: \\treturn fExtenders, nil\\n521: }\\n522: \\n523: type \\n525: func unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile internalqueue.QueueingHintMapPerProfile) map[framework.GVK]framework.ActionType {\\n526: \\tgvkMap := = podQueue.Pop\\n357: \\tsched.applyDefaultHandlers()\\n358: \\n359: \\tif err = addAllEventHandlers(sched, informerFactory, dynInformerFactory, unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile)); err != nil {\\n360: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"adding event handlers: %w\\\", err)\\n361: \\t}\\n362: \\n363: \\treturn sched, nil\\n364: }\\n365: \\n366: // defaultQueueingHintFn is the default queueing hint function.\\n367: // It always returns Queue as the queueing hint.\\n368: var defaultQueueingHintFn = func(_ klog.Logger, _ *v1.Pod, _, _ interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n369: \\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n370: }\\n371: \\n372: func buildQueueingHintMap(es []framework.EnqueueExtensions) internalqueue.QueueingHintMap {\\n373: \\tqueueingHintMap := make(internalqueue.QueueingHintMap)\\n374: \\tfor _, e := range es {\\n375: \\t\\tevents := e.EventsToRegister()\\n376: \\n377: \\t\\t// This will happen when plugin registers with empty events, it's usually the case a pod\\n378: \\t\\t// will become reschedulable only for self-update, e.g. schedulingGates plugin, the pod\\n379: \\t\\t// will enter into the activeQ via priorityQueue.Update().\\n380: \\t\\tif len(events) == 0 {\\n381: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n382: \\t\\t}\\n383: \\n384: \\t\\t// Note: Rarely, a plugin implements EnqueueExtensions but returns nil.\\n385: \\t\\t// We treat it as: the plugin is not interested in any event, and hence pod failed by that plugin\\n386: \\t\\t// cannot be moved by any regular cluster event.\\n387: \\t\\t// So, we can just ignore such EventsToRegister here.\\n388: \\n389: \\t\\tregisterNodeAdded := false\\n390: \\t\\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated := false\\n391: \\t\\tfor _, event := range events {\\n392: \\t\\t\\tfn := event.QueueingHintFn\\n393: \\t\\t\\tif fn == nil || !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SchedulerQueueingHints) {\\n394: \\t\\t\\t\\tfn = defaultQueueingHintFn\\n395: \\t\\t\\t}\\n396: \\n397: \\t\\t\\tif event.Event.Resource == framework.Node {\\n398: \\t\\t\\t\\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.Add != 0 {\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tregisterNodeAdded = true\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n401: \\t\\t\\t\\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.UpdateNodeTaint != 0 {\\n402: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated = true\\n403: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n404: \\t\\t\\t}\\n405: \\n406: \\t\\t\\tqueueingHintMap[event.Event] = append(queueingHintMap[event.Event], &internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\\n407: \\t\\t\\t\\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\\n408: \\t\\t\\t\\tQueueingHintFn: fn,\\n409: \\t\\t\\t})\\n410: \\t\\t}\\n411: \\t\\tif registerNodeAdded && !registerNodeTaintUpdated {\\n412: \\t\\t\\t// Temporally fix for the issue https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/109437\\n413: \\t\\t\\t// NodeAdded QueueingHint isn't always called because of preCheck.\\n414: \\t\\t\\t// It's definitely not something expected for plugin developers,\\n415: \\t\\t\\t// and registering UpdateNodeTaint event is the only mitigation for now.\\n416: \\t\\t\\t//\\n417: \\t\\t\\t// So, here registers UpdateNodeTaint event for plugins that has NodeAdded event, but don't have UpdateNodeTaint event.\\n418: \\t\\t\\t// It has a bad impact for the requeuing efficiency though, a lot better than some Pods being stuch in the\\n419: \\t\\t\\t// unschedulable pod pool.\\n420: \\t\\t\\t// This behavior will be removed when we remove the preCheck feature.\\n421: \\t\\t\\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\\n422: \\t\\t\\tqueueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}] =\\n423: \\t\\t\\t\\tappend(queueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}],\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t&internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\\n425: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tQueueingHintFn: defaultQueueingHintFn,\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n428: \\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n429: \\t\\t}\\n430: \\t}\\n431: \\treturn queueingHintMap\\n432: }\\n433: \\n434: // Run begins watching and scheduling. It starts scheduling and blocked until the context is done.\\n435: func (sched *Scheduler) Run(ctx context.Context) {\\n436: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n437: \\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Run(logger)\\n438: \\n439: \\t// We need to start scheduleOne loop in a dedicated goroutine,\\n440: \\t// because scheduleOne function hangs on getting the next item\\n441: \\t// from the SchedulingQueue.\\n442: \\t// If there are no new pods to schedule, it will be hanging there\\n443: \\t// and if done in this goroutine it will be blocking closing\\n444: \\t// SchedulingQueue, in effect causing a deadlock on shutdown.\\n445: \\tgo wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, sched.ScheduleOne, 0)\\n446: \\n447: \\t<-ctx.Done()\\n448: \\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Close()\\n449: \\n450: \\t// If the plugins satisfy the io.Closer interface, they are closed.\\n451: \\terr := sched.Profiles.Close()\\n452: \\tif err != nil {\\n453: \\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Failed to close plugins\\\")\\n454: \\t}\\n455: }\\n456: \\n457: // NewInformerFactory creates a SharedInformerFactory and initializes a scheduler specific\\n458: // in-place podInformer.\\n459: func \\tinformerFactory.InformerFor(&v1.Pod{}, newPodInformer)\\n462: \\treturn informerFactory\\n463: }\\n464: \\n465: func buildExtenders(logger klog.Logger, extenders []schedulerapi.Extender, profiles \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n511: \\t\\t\\t\\targs.IgnoredResources = ignoredExtendedResources\\n512: \\t\\t\\t\\tfound = true\\n513: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n514: \\t\\t\\t}\\n515: \\t\\t}\\n516: \\t\\tif !found {\\n517: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"can't find NodeResourcesFitArgs in plugin config\\\")\\n518: \\t\\t}\\n519: \\t}\\n520: \\treturn fExtenders, nil\\n521: }\\n522: \\n523: type \\n525: func unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile internalqueue.QueueingHintMapPerProfile) map[framework.GVK]framework.ActionType {\\n526: \\tgvkMap := It attempts to find\n63: // nodes that they fit on and writes bindings back to the api server.\n64: type Scheduler struct {\n65: \t// It is expected that changes made via Cache will be observed\n66: \t// by NodeLister and Algorithm.\n67: \tCache internalcache.Cache\n68: \n69: \tExtenders []framework.Extender\n70: \n71: \t// NextPod should be a function that blocks until the next pod\n72: \t// is available. }\n371: \n372: func buildQueueingHintMap(es []framework.EnqueueExtensions) internalqueue.QueueingHintMap {\n373: \tqueueingHintMap := make(internalqueue.QueueingHintMap)\n374: \tfor _, e := range es {\n375: \t\tevents := e.EventsToRegister()\n376: \n377: \t\t// This will happen when plugin registers with empty events, it's usually the case a pod\n378: \t\t// will become reschedulable only for self-update, e.g. schedulingGates plugin, the pod\n379: \t\t// will enter into the activeQ via priorityQueue.Update().\n380: \t\tif len(events) == 0 {\n381: \t\t\tcontinue\n382: \t\t}\n383: \n384: \t\t// Note: Rarely, a plugin implements EnqueueExtensions but returns nil.\n385: \t\t// We treat it as: the plugin is not interested in any event, and hence pod failed by that plugin\n386: \t\t// cannot be moved by any regular cluster event.\n387: \t\t// So, we can just ignore such EventsToRegister here.\n388: \n389: \t\tregisterNodeAdded := false\n390: \t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated := false\n391: \t\tfor _, event := range events {\n392: \t\t\tfn := event.QueueingHintFn\n393: \t\t\tif fn == nil || !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SchedulerQueueingHints) {\n394: \t\t\t\tfn = defaultQueueingHintFn\n395: \t\t\t}\n396: \n397: \t\t\tif event.Event.Resource == framework.Node {\n398: \t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.Add != 0 {\n399: \t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeAdded = true\n400: \t\t\t\t}\n401: \t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.UpdateNodeTaint != 0 {\n402: \t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated = true\n403: \t\t\t\t}\n404: \t\t\t}\n405: \n406: \t\t\tqueueingHintMap[event.Event] = append(queueingHintMap[event.Event], &internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n407: \t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n408: \t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: fn,\n409: \t\t\t})\n410: \t\t}\n411: \t\tif registerNodeAdded && !registerNodeTaintUpdated {\n412: \t\t\t// Temporally fix for the issue https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/109437\n413: \t\t\t// NodeAdded QueueingHint isn't always called because of preCheck.\n414: \t\t\t// It's definitely not something expected for plugin developers,\n415: \t\t\t// and registering UpdateNodeTaint event is the only mitigation for now.\n416: \t\t\t//\n417: \t\t\t// So, here registers UpdateNodeTaint event for plugins that has NodeAdded event, but don't have UpdateNodeTaint event.\n418: \t\t\t// It has a bad impact for the requeuing efficiency though, a lot better than some Pods being stuch in the\n419: \t\t\t// unschedulable pod pool.\n420: \t\t\t// This behavior will be removed when we remove the preCheck feature.\n421: \t\t\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n422: \t\t\tqueueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}] =\n423: \t\t\t\tappend(queueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}],\n424: \t\t\t\t\t&internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n425: \t\t\t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n426: \t\t\t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: defaultQueueingHintFn,\n427: \t\t\t\t\t},\n428: \t\t\t\t)\n429: \t\t}\n430: \t}\n431: \treturn queueingHintMap\n432: }\n433: \n434: // Run begins watching and scheduling. It starts scheduling and blocked until the context is done.\n435: func (sched *Scheduler) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n436: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n437: \tsched.SchedulingQueue.Run(logger)\n438: \n439: \t// We need to start scheduleOne loop in a dedicated goroutine,\n440: \t// because scheduleOne function hangs on getting the next item\n441: \t// from the SchedulingQueue.\n442: \t// If there are no new pods to schedule, it will be hanging there\n443: \t// and if done in this goroutine it will be blocking closing\n444: \t// SchedulingQueue, in effect causing a deadlock on shutdown.\n445: \tgo wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, sched.ScheduleOne, 0)\n446: \n447: \t<-ctx.Done()\n448: \tsched.SchedulingQueue.Close()\n449: \n450: \t// If the plugins satisfy the io.Closer interface, they are closed.\n451: \terr := sched.Profiles.Close()\n452: \tif err != nil {\n453: \t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to close plugins\")\n454: \t}\n455: }\n456: \n457: {\n466: \tvar fExtenders []framework.Extender\n467: \tif len(extenders) == 0 {\n468: \t\treturn nil, nil\n469: \t}\n470: \n471: \tvar ignoredExtendedResources []string\n472: \tvar ignorableExtenders describe the error that no nodes available to schedule pods.\nvar ErrNoNodesAvailable = fmt.Errorf(\"no nodes available to schedule pods\")\n\n// Scheduler watches for new unscheduled pods. It attempts to find\n// nodes that they fit on and writes bindings back to the api server.\ntype Scheduler struct {\n\t// It is expected that changes made via Cache will be observed\n\t// by NodeLister and Algorithm.\n\tCache internalcache.Cache\n\n\tExtenders []framework.Extender\n\n\t// NextPod should be a function that blocks until the next pod\n\t// is available. We don't use a channel for this, because scheduling\n\t// a pod may take some amount of time and we don't want pods to get\n\t// stale while they sit in a channel.\n\tNextPod func(logger klog.Logger) (*framework.QueuedPodInfo, error)\n\n\t// FailureHandler is called upon a scheduling failure.\n\tFailureHandler FailureHandlerFn\n\n\t// SchedulePod tries to schedule the given pod to one of the nodes in the node list.\n\t// Return a struct of ScheduleResult with the name of suggested host on success,\n\t// otherwise will return a FitError with reasons.\n\tSchedulePod func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (ScheduleResult, error)\n\n\t// Close this to shut down the scheduler.\n\tStopEverything <-chan struct{}\n\n\t// SchedulingQueue holds pods to be scheduled\n\tSchedulingQueue internalqueue.SchedulingQueue\n\n\t// Profiles are the scheduling profiles.\n\tProfiles profile.Map\n\n\tclient clientset.Interface\n\n\tnodeInfoSnapshot *internalcache.Snapshot\n\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore int32\n\n\tnextStartNodeIndex int\n\n\t// logger *must* be initialized when creating a Scheduler,\n\t// otherwise logging functions will access a nil sink and\n\t// panic.\n\tlogger klog.Logger\n\n\t// registeredHandlers contains the registrations of all handlers. Therefore, we delay creating it until New is actually\n\t// invoked.\n\tapplyDefaultProfile: true,\n}\n\n// New returns a Scheduler\nfunc New(ctx context.Context,\n\tclient ...Option) (*Scheduler, error) {\n\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tstopEverything := ctx.Done()\n\n\toptions := defaultSchedulerOptions\n\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n\t\topt(&options)\n\t}\n\n\tif options.applyDefaultProfile {\n\t\tvar versionedCfg configv1.KubeSchedulerConfiguration\n\t\tscheme.Scheme.Default(&versionedCfg)\n\t\tcfg := schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerConfiguration{}\n\t\tif err := scheme.Scheme.Convert(&versionedCfg, &cfg, nil); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\toptions.profiles = cfg.Profiles\n\t}\n\n\tregistry := frameworkplugins.NewInTreeRegistry()\n\tif err := registry.Merge(options.frameworkOutOfTreeRegistry); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.Register()\n\n\textenders, err := buildExtenders(logger, options.extenders, options.profiles)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"couldn't build extenders: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tpodLister := informerFactory.Core().V1().Pods().Lister()\n\tnodeLister := informerFactory.Core().V1().Nodes().Lister()\n\n\tsnapshot := internalcache.NewEmptySnapshot()\n\tmetricsRecorder := metrics.NewMetricsAsyncRecorder(1000, time.Second, stopEverything)\n\n\tprofiles, err := profile.NewMap(ctx, options.profiles, registry, recorderFactory,\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithComponentConfigVersion(options.componentConfigVersion),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithClientSet(client),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithKubeConfig(options.kubeConfig),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithInformerFactory(informerFactory),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithSnapshotSharedLister(snapshot),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithCaptureProfile(frameworkruntime.CaptureProfile(options.frameworkCapturer)),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithParallelism(int(options.parallelism)),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithExtenders(extenders),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithMetricsRecorder(metricsRecorder),\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"initializing profiles: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(profiles) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"at least one profile is required\")\n\t}\n\n\tpreEnqueuePluginMap := make(map[string][]framework.PreEnqueuePlugin)\n\tqueueingHintsPerProfile := make(internalqueue.QueueingHintMapPerProfile)\n\tfor profileName, profile := range profiles {\n\t\tpreEnqueuePluginMap[profileName] = profile.PreEnqueuePlugins()\n\t\tqueueingHintsPerProfile[profileName] = buildQueueingHintMap(profile.EnqueueExtensions())\n\t}\n\n\tpodQueue := internalqueue.NewSchedulingQueue(\n\t\tprofiles[options.profiles[0].SchedulerName].QueueSortFunc(),\n\t\tinformerFactory,\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodInitialBackoffDuration(time.Duration(options.podInitialBackoffSeconds)*time.Second),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodMaxBackoffDuration(time.Duration(options.podMaxBackoffSeconds)*time.Second),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodLister(podLister),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration(options.podMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPreEnqueuePluginMap(preEnqueuePluginMap),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithQueueingHintMapPerProfile(queueingHintsPerProfile),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPluginMetricsSamplePercent(pluginMetricsSamplePercent),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithMetricsRecorder(*metricsRecorder),\n\t)\n\n\tfor _, fwk := range profiles {\n\t\tfwk.SetPodNominator(podQueue)\n\t}\n\n\tschedulerCache := internalcache.New(ctx, durationToExpireAssumedPod)\n\n\t// Setup cache debugger.\n\tdebugger := cachedebugger.New(nodeLister, podLister, schedulerCache, podQueue)\n\tdebugger.ListenForSignal(ctx)\n\n\tsched := &Scheduler{\n\t\tCache:                    schedulerCache,\n\t\tclient:                   client,\n\t\tnodeInfoSnapshot:         snapshot,\n\t\tpercentageOfNodesToScore: options.percentageOfNodesToScore,\n\t\tExtenders:                extenders,\n\t\tStopEverything:           stopEverything,\n\t\tSchedulingQueue:          podQueue,\n\t\tProfiles:                 profiles,\n\t\tlogger:                   logger,\n\t}\n\tsched.NextPod = podQueue.Pop\n\tsched.applyDefaultHandlers()\n\n\tif err = addAllEventHandlers(sched, informerFactory, dynInformerFactory, unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile)); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"adding event handlers: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn sched, nil\n}\n\n// defaultQueueingHintFn is the default queueing hint function.\n// It always returns Queue as the queueing hint.\nvar defaultQueueingHintFn = func(_ klog.Logger, _ *v1.Pod, _, _ interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n}\n\nfunc buildQueueingHintMap(es []framework.EnqueueExtensions) internalqueue.QueueingHintMap {\n\tqueueingHintMap := make(internalqueue.QueueingHintMap)\n\tfor _, e := range es {\n\t\tevents := e.EventsToRegister()\n\n\t\t// This will happen when plugin registers with empty events, it's usually the case a pod\n\t\t// will become reschedulable only for self-update, e.g. schedulingGates plugin, the pod\n\t\t// will enter into the activeQ via priorityQueue.Update().\n\t\tif len(events) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Note: Rarely, a plugin implements EnqueueExtensions but returns nil.\n\t\t// We treat it as: the plugin is not interested in any event, and hence pod failed by that plugin\n\t\t// cannot be moved by any regular cluster event.\n\t\t// So, we can just ignore such EventsToRegister here.\n\n\t\tregisterNodeAdded := false\n\t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated := false\n\t\tfor _, event := range events {\n\t\t\tfn := event.QueueingHintFn\n\t\t\tif fn == nil || !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SchedulerQueueingHints) {\n\t\t\t\tfn = defaultQueueingHintFn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif event.Event.Resource == framework.Node {\n\t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.Add != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeAdded = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.UpdateNodeTaint != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tqueueingHintMap[event.Event] = append(queueingHintMap[event.Event], &internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n\t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n\t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: fn,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t\tif registerNodeAdded && !registerNodeTaintUpdated {\n\t\t\t// Temporally fix for the issue https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/109437\n\t\t\t// NodeAdded QueueingHint isn't always called because of preCheck.\n\t\t\t// It's definitely not something expected for plugin developers,\n\t\t\t// and registering UpdateNodeTaint event is the only mitigation for now.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// So, here registers UpdateNodeTaint event for plugins that has NodeAdded event, but don't have UpdateNodeTaint event.\n\t\t\t// It has a bad impact for the requeuing efficiency though, a lot better than some Pods being stuch in the\n\t\t\t// unschedulable pod pool.\n\t\t\t// This behavior will be removed when we remove the preCheck feature.\n\t\t\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\t\t\tqueueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}] =\n\t\t\t\tappend(queueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}],\n\t\t\t\t\t&internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: defaultQueueingHintFn,\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn queueingHintMap\n}\n\n// Run begins watching and scheduling. It starts scheduling and blocked until the context is done.\nfunc (sched *Scheduler) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Run(logger)\n\n\t// We need to start scheduleOne loop in a dedicated goroutine,\n\t// because scheduleOne function hangs on getting the next item\n\t// from the SchedulingQueue.\n\t// If there are no new pods to schedule, it will be hanging there\n\t// and if done in this goroutine it will be blocking closing\n\t// SchedulingQueue, in effect causing a deadlock on shutdown.\n\tgo wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, sched.ScheduleOne, 0)\n\n\t<-ctx.Done()\n\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Close()\n\n\t// If the plugins satisfy the io.Closer interface, they are closed.\n\terr := sched.Profiles.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to close plugins\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewInformerFactory creates a SharedInformerFactory and initializes a scheduler specific\n// in-place podInformer.\nfunc NewInformerFactory(cs clientset.Interface, resyncPeriod time.Duration) informers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\tinformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(cs, resyncPeriod)\n\tinformerFactory.InformerFor(&v1.Pod{}, newPodInformer)\n\treturn informerFactory\n}\n\nfunc buildExtenders(logger klog.Logger, extenders []schedulerapi.Extender, profiles []schedulerapi.KubeSchedulingProfile) ([]framework.Extender, error) {\n\tvar fExtenders []framework.Extender\n\tif len(extenders) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar ignoredExtendedResources []string\n\tvar ignorableExtenders []framework.Extender\n\tfor i := range extenders {\n\t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Creating extender\", \"extender\", extenders[i])\n\t\textender, err := NewHTTPExtender(&extenders[i])\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !extender.IsIgnorable() {\n\t\t\tfExtenders = append(fExtenders, extender)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tignorableExtenders = append(ignorableExtenders, extender)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, r := range extenders[i].ManagedResources {\n\t\t\tif r.IgnoredByScheduler {\n\t\t\t\tignoredExtendedResources = append(ignoredExtendedResources, r.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// place ignorable extenders to the tail of extenders\n\tfExtenders = append(fExtenders, ignorableExtenders...)\n\n\t// If there are any extended resources found from the Extenders, append them to the pluginConfig for each profile.\n\t// This should only have an effect on ComponentConfig, where it is possible to configure Extenders and\n\t// plugin args (and in which case the extender ignored resources take precedence).\n\tif len(ignoredExtendedResources) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fExtenders, nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range profiles {\n\t\tprof := &profiles[i]\n\t\tvar found = false\n\t\tfor k := range prof.PluginConfig {\n\t\t\tif prof.PluginConfig[k].Name == noderesources.Name {\n\t\t\t\t// Update the existing args\n\t\t\t\tpc := &prof.PluginConfig[k]\n\t\t\t\targs, ok := pc.Args.(*schedulerapi.NodeResourcesFitArgs)\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesFitArgs, got %T\", pc.Args)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\targs.IgnoredResources = ignoredExtendedResources\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"can't find NodeResourcesFitArgs in plugin config\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn fExtenders, nil\n}\n\ntype FailureHandlerFn func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, podInfo *framework.QueuedPodInfo, status *framework.Status, nominatingInfo *framework.NominatingInfo, start time.Time)\n\nfunc unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile internalqueue.QueueingHintMapPerProfile) map[framework.GVK]framework.ActionType {\n\tgvkMap := make(map[framework.GVK]framework.ActionType)\n\tfor _, queueingHints := range queueingHintsPerProfile {\n\t\tfor evt := range queueingHints {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := gvkMap[evt.Resource]; ok {\n\t\t\t\tgvkMap[evt.Resource] |= evt.ActionType\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tgvkMap[evt.Resource] = evt.ActionType\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn gvkMap\n}\n\n// newPodInformer creates a shared index informer that returns only non-terminal pods.\n// The PodInformer allows indexers to be added, but note that only non-conflict indexers are allowed.\nfunc newPodInformer(cs clientset.Interface, resyncPeriod time.Duration) cache.SharedIndexInformer {\n\tselector := fmt.Sprintf(\"status.phase!=%v,status.phase!=%v\", v1.PodSucceeded, v1.PodFailed)\n\ttweakListOptions := func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {\n\t\toptions.FieldSelector = selector\n\t}\n\tinformer := coreinformers.NewFilteredPodInformer(cs, metav1.NamespaceAll, resyncPeriod, cache.Indexers{}, tweakListOptions)\n\n\t// Dropping `.metadata.managedFields` to improve memory usage.\n\t// The Extract workflow (i.e. `ExtractPod`) should be unused.\n\ttrim := func(obj interface{}) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif accessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {\n\t\t\taccessor.SetManagedFields(nil)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn obj, nil\n\t}\n\tinformer.SetTransform(trim)\n\treturn describe the error that no nodes available to schedule pods.\nvar ErrNoNodesAvailable = fmt.Errorf(\"no nodes available to schedule pods\")\n\n// Scheduler watches for new unscheduled pods. It attempts to find\n// nodes that they fit on and writes bindings back to the api server.\ntype Scheduler struct {\n\t// It is expected that changes made via Cache will be observed\n\t// by NodeLister and Algorithm.\n\tCache internalcache.Cache\n\n\tExtenders []framework.Extender\n\n\t// NextPod should be a function that blocks until the next pod\n\t// is available. We don't use a channel for this, because scheduling\n\t// a pod may take some amount of time and we don't want pods to get\n\t// stale while they sit in a channel.\n\tNextPod func(logger klog.Logger) (*framework.QueuedPodInfo, error)\n\n\t// FailureHandler is called upon a scheduling failure.\n\tFailureHandler FailureHandlerFn\n\n\t// SchedulePod tries to schedule the given pod to one of the nodes in the node list.\n\t// Return a struct of ScheduleResult with the name of suggested host on success,\n\t// otherwise will return a FitError with reasons.\n\tSchedulePod func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (ScheduleResult, error)\n\n\t// Close this to shut down the scheduler.\n\tStopEverything <-chan struct{}\n\n\t// SchedulingQueue holds pods to be scheduled\n\tSchedulingQueue internalqueue.SchedulingQueue\n\n\t// Profiles are the scheduling profiles.\n\tProfiles profile.Map\n\n\tclient clientset.Interface\n\n\tnodeInfoSnapshot *internalcache.Snapshot\n\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore int32\n\n\tnextStartNodeIndex int\n\n\t// logger *must* be initialized when creating a Scheduler,\n\t// otherwise logging functions will access a nil sink and\n\t// panic.\n\tlogger klog.Logger\n\n\t// registeredHandlers contains the registrations of all handlers. It's used to check if all handlers have finished syncing before the scheduling cycles start.\n\tregisteredHandlers []cache.ResourceEventHandlerRegistration\n}\n\nfunc (sched *Scheduler) applyDefaultHandlers() {\n\tsched.SchedulePod = sched.schedulePod\n\tsched.FailureHandler = sched.handleSchedulingFailure\n}\n\ntype schedulerOptions struct {\n\tcomponentConfigVersion string\n\tkubeConfig             *restclient.Config\n\t// Overridden by profile level percentageOfNodesToScore if set in v1.\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore          int32\n\tpodInitialBackoffSeconds          int64\n\tpodMaxBackoffSeconds              int64\n\tpodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration time.Duration\n\t// Contains out-of-tree plugins to be merged with the in-tree registry.\n\tframeworkOutOfTreeRegistry frameworkruntime.Registry\n\tprofiles                   []schedulerapi.KubeSchedulingProfile\n\textenders                  []schedulerapi.Extender\n\tframeworkCapturer          FrameworkCapturer\n\tparallelism                int32\n\tapplyDefaultProfile        bool\n}\n\n// Option configures a Scheduler\ntype Option func(*schedulerOptions)\n\n// ScheduleResult represents the result of scheduling a pod.\ntype ScheduleResult struct {\n\t// Name of the selected node.\n\tSuggestedHost string\n\t// The number of nodes the scheduler evaluated the pod against in the filtering\n\t// phase and beyond.\n\t// Note that it contains the number of nodes that filtered out by PreFilterResult.\n\tEvaluatedNodes int\n\t// The number of nodes out of the evaluated ones that fit the pod.\n\tFeasibleNodes int\n\t// The nominating info for scheduling cycle.\n\tnominatingInfo *framework.NominatingInfo\n}\n\n// WithComponentConfigVersion sets the component config version to the\n// KubeSchedulerConfiguration version used. The string should be the full\n// scheme group/version of the external type we converted from (for example\n// \"kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\")\nfunc WithComponentConfigVersion(apiVersion string) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.componentConfigVersion = apiVersion\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithKubeConfig sets the kube config for Scheduler.\nfunc WithKubeConfig(cfg *restclient.Config) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.kubeConfig = cfg\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithProfiles sets profiles for Scheduler. By default, there is one profile\n// with the name \"default-scheduler\".\nfunc WithProfiles(p ...schedulerapi.KubeSchedulingProfile) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.profiles = p\n\t\to.applyDefaultProfile = false\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithParallelism sets the parallelism for all scheduler algorithms. Default is 16.\nfunc WithParallelism(threads int32) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.parallelism = threads\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPercentageOfNodesToScore sets percentageOfNodesToScore for Scheduler.\n// The default value of 0 will use an adaptive percentage: 50 - (num of nodes)/125.\nfunc WithPercentageOfNodesToScore(percentageOfNodesToScore *int32) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\tif percentageOfNodesToScore != nil {\n\t\t\to.percentageOfNodesToScore = *percentageOfNodesToScore\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry sets the registry for out-of-tree plugins. Those plugins\n// will be appended to the default registry.\nfunc WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry frameworkruntime.Registry) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.frameworkOutOfTreeRegistry = registry\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds sets podInitialBackoffSeconds for Scheduler, the default value is 1\nfunc WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds(podInitialBackoffSeconds int64) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.podInitialBackoffSeconds = podInitialBackoffSeconds\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds sets podMaxBackoffSeconds for Scheduler, the default value is 10\nfunc WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds(podMaxBackoffSeconds int64) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) int64(internalqueue.DefaultPodInitialBackoffDuration.Seconds()),\n\tpodMaxBackoffSeconds: int32(parallelize.DefaultParallelism),\n\t// Ideally we would statically set the default profile here, but we can't because\n\t// creating the default profile may require testing feature gates, which may get\n\t// set dynamically in tests. Therefore, we delay creating it until New is actually\n\t// invoked.\n\tapplyDefaultProfile: true,\n}\n\n// New returns a Scheduler\nfunc New(ctx context.Context,\n\tclient schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerConfiguration{}\n\t\tif err := scheme.Scheme.Convert(&versionedCfg, &cfg, nil); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\toptions.profiles = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.Register()\n\n\textenders, err := buildExtenders(logger, options.extenders, options.profiles)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"couldn't build extenders: %w\", internalcache.NewEmptySnapshot()\n\tmetricsRecorder := metrics.NewMetricsAsyncRecorder(1000, time.Second, stopEverything)\n\n\tprofiles, err := profile.NewMap(ctx, options.profiles, registry, recorderFactory,\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithComponentConfigVersion(options.componentConfigVersion),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithClientSet(client),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithKubeConfig(options.kubeConfig),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithInformerFactory(informerFactory),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithSnapshotSharedLister(snapshot),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithCaptureProfile(frameworkruntime.CaptureProfile(options.frameworkCapturer)),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithParallelism(int(options.parallelism)),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithExtenders(extenders),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithMetricsRecorder(metricsRecorder),\n\t)\n\tif defaultQueueingHintFn = func(_ klog.Logger, _ *v1.Pod, _, _ interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n}\n\nfunc buildQueueingHintMap(es []framework.EnqueueExtensions) internalqueue.QueueingHintMap {\n\tqueueingHintMap := make(internalqueue.QueueingHintMap)\n\tfor _, e := range es {\n\t\tevents := e.EventsToRegister()\n\n\t\t// This will happen when plugin registers with empty events, it's usually the case a pod\n\t\t// will become reschedulable only for self-update, e.g. schedulingGates plugin, the pod\n\t\t// will enter into the activeQ via priorityQueue.Update().\n\t\tif len(events) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Note: Rarely, a plugin implements EnqueueExtensions but returns nil.\n\t\t// We treat it as: the plugin is not interested in any event, and hence pod failed by that plugin\n\t\t// cannot be moved by any regular cluster event.\n\t\t// So, we can just ignore such EventsToRegister here.\n\n\t\tregisterNodeAdded := false\n\t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated := false\n\t\tfor _, event := range events {\n\t\t\tfn := event.QueueingHintFn\n\t\t\tif fn == nil || !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SchedulerQueueingHints) {\n\t\t\t\tfn = defaultQueueingHintFn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif event.Event.Resource == framework.Node {\n\t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.Add != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeAdded = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.UpdateNodeTaint != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tqueueingHintMap[event.Event] = append(queueingHintMap[event.Event], &internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n\t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n\t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: fn,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t\tif registerNodeAdded && !registerNodeTaintUpdated {\n\t\t\t// Temporally fix for the issue https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/109437\n\t\t\t// NodeAdded QueueingHint isn't always called because of preCheck.\n\t\t\t// It's definitely not something expected for plugin developers,\n\t\t\t// and registering UpdateNodeTaint event is the only mitigation for now.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// So, here registers UpdateNodeTaint event for plugins that has NodeAdded event, but don't have UpdateNodeTaint event.\n\t\t\t// It has a bad impact for the requeuing efficiency though, a lot better than some Pods being stuch in the\n\t\t\t// unschedulable pod pool.\n\t\t\t// This behavior will be removed when we remove the preCheck feature.\n\t\t\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\t\t\tqueueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}] =\n\t\t\t\tappend(queueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}],\n\t\t\t\t\t&internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: defaultQueueingHintFn,\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn queueingHintMap\n}\n\n// Run begins watching and scheduling. It starts scheduling and blocked until the context is done.\nfunc (sched *Scheduler) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Run(logger)\n\n\t// We need to start scheduleOne loop in a dedicated goroutine,\n\t// because scheduleOne function hangs on getting the next item\n\t// from the SchedulingQueue.\n\t// If there are no new pods to schedule, it will be hanging there\n\t// and if done in this goroutine it will be blocking closing\n\t// SchedulingQueue, in effect causing a deadlock on shutdown.\n\tgo wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, sched.ScheduleOne, 0)\n\n\t<-ctx.Done()\n\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Close()\n\n\t// If the plugins satisfy the io.Closer interface, they are closed.\n\terr := sched.Profiles.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to close plugins\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewInformerFactory creates a SharedInformerFactory and initializes a scheduler specific\n// in-place podInformer.\nfunc NewInformerFactory(cs clientset.Interface, resyncPeriod time.Duration) informers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\tinformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(cs, resyncPeriod)\n\tinformerFactory.InformerFor(&v1.Pod{}, newPodInformer)\n\treturn informerFactory\n}\n\nfunc buildExtenders(logger klog.Logger, extenders []schedulerapi.Extender, profiles []schedulerapi.KubeSchedulingProfile) ([]framework.Extender, error) {\n\tvar fExtenders []framework.Extender\n\tif len(extenders) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar ignoredExtendedResources []string\n\tvar ignorableExtenders []framework.Extender\n\tfor i := range extenders {\n\t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Creating extender\", \"extender\", extenders[i])\n\t\textender, err := NewHTTPExtender(&extenders[i])\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !extender.IsIgnorable() {\n\t\t\tfExtenders = append(fExtenders, extender)\n\t\t} else append(ignoredExtendedResources, r.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// place ignorable extenders to the tail of extenders\n\tfExtenders = append(fExtenders, ignorableExtenders...)\n\n\t// If there are and\n\t// plugin args (and in which case the extender ignored resources take precedence).\n\tif len(ignoredExtendedResources) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fExtenders, nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range profiles pc.Args)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\targs.IgnoredResources = ignoredExtendedResources\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"can't find NodeResourcesFitArgs in plugin config\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn fExtenders, nil\n}\n\ntype FailureHandlerFn func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, podInfo *framework.QueuedPodInfo, status *framework.Status, nominatingInfo *framework.NominatingInfo, start time.Time)\n\nfunc unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile internalqueue.QueueingHintMapPerProfile) interface.\n414: func (f *frameworkImpl) Close() error {\n415: \tvar errs []error\n416: \tfor name, plugin := range f.pluginsMap {\n417: \t\tif closer, ok := plugin.(io.Closer); ok {\n418: \t\t\terr := closer.Close()\n419: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n420: \t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"%s failed to close: %w\", name, err))\n421: \t\t\t\t// We try to close all plugins even if we got errors from some.\n422: \t\t\t}\n423: \t\t}\n424: \t}\n425: \treturn errors.Join(errs...)\n426: }\n427: \n428: // getScoreWeights makes sure that, between MultiPoint-Score plugin weights and individual Score\n429: // plugin weights there is not an overflow of MaxTotalScore.\n430: func getScoreWeights(f *frameworkImpl, plugins []config.Plugin) error {\n431: \tvar totalPriority int64\n432: \tscorePlugins := reflect.ValueOf(&f.scorePlugins).Elem()\n433: \tpluginType := scorePlugins.Type().Elem()\n434: \tfor _, e := range plugins {\n435: \t\tpg := f.pluginsMap[e.Name]\n436: \t\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\n437: \t\t\tcontinue\n438: \t\t}\n439: \n440: \t\t// We append MultiPoint plugins to the list of Score plugins. So if this plugin has already been\n441: \t\t// encountered, let the individual Score weight take precedence.\n442: \t\tif _, ok := f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]; ok {\n443: \t\t\tcontinue\n444: \t\t}\n445: \t\t// a weight of zero is not permitted, plugins can be disabled explicitly\n446: \t\t// when configured.\n447: \t\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = int(e.Weight)\n448: \t\tif f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] == 0 {\n449: \t\t\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = 1\n450: \t\t}\n451: \n452: \t\t// Checks totalPriority against MaxTotalScore to avoid overflow\n453: \t\tif int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name])*framework.MaxNodeScore > framework.MaxTotalScore-totalPriority {\n454: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"total score of Score plugins could overflow\")\n455: \t\t}\n456: \t\ttotalPriority += int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]) * framework.MaxNodeScore\n457: \t}\n458: \treturn nil\n459: }\n460: \n461: type orderedSet struct {\n462: \tset         map[string]int\n463: \tlist        []string\n464: \tdeletionCnt int\n465: }\n466: \n467: func newOrderedSet() *orderedSet {\n468: \treturn &orderedSet{set: make(map[string]int)}\n469: }\n470: \n471: func (os *orderedSet) insert(s string) {\n472: \tif os.has(s) {\n473: \t\treturn\n474: \t}\n475: \tos.set[s] = len(os.list)\n476: \tos.list = append(os.list, s)\n477: }\n478: \n479: func (os *orderedSet) has(s string) bool {\n480: \t_, found := os.set[s]\n481: \treturn found\n482: }\n483: \n484: func (os *orderedSet) delete(s string) {\n485: \tif i, found := os.set[s]; found {\n486: \t\tdelete(os.set, s)\n487: \t\tos.list = append(os.list[:i-os.deletionCnt], os.list[i+1-os.deletionCnt:]...)\n488: \t\tos.deletionCnt++\n489: \t}\n490: }\n491: \n492: func (f *frameworkImpl) expandMultiPointPlugins(logger klog.Logger, profile *config.KubeSchedulerProfile) error {\n493: \t// initialize MultiPoint plugins\n494: \tfor _, e := range f.getExtensionPoints(profile.Plugins) {\n495: \t\tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()\n496: \t\tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\n497: \t\t// build enabledSet of plugins already registered via normal extension points\n498: \t\t// to check double registration\n499: \t\tenabledSet := newOrderedSet()\n500: \t\tfor _, plugin := range e.plugins.Enabled {\n501: \t\t\tenabledSet.insert(plugin.Name)\n502: \t\t}\n503: \n504: \t\tdisabledSet := sets.New[string]()\n505: \t\tfor _, disabledPlugin := range e.plugins.Disabled {\n506: \t\t\tdisabledSet.Insert(disabledPlugin.Name)\n507: \t\t}\n508: \t\tif disabledSet.Has(\"*\") {\n509: \t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Skipped MultiPoint expansion because all plugins are disabled for extension point\", \"extension\", pluginType)\n510: \t\t\tcontinue\n511: \t\t}\n512: \n513: \t\t// track plugins enabled via multipoint separately from those enabled by specific extensions,\n514: \t\t// so that we can distinguish between double-registration and explicit overrides\n515: \t\tmultiPointEnabled := newOrderedSet()\n516: \t\toverridePlugins := newOrderedSet()\n517: \t\tfor _, ep := range profile.Plugins.MultiPoint.Enabled {\n518: \t\t\tpg, ok := f.pluginsMap[ep.Name]\n519: \t\t\tif !ok {\n520: \t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s %q does not exist\", pluginType.Name(), ep.Name)\n521: \t\t\t}\n522: \n523: \t\t\t// if this plugin doesn't implement the type for the current extension we're trying to expand, skip\n524: \t\t\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\n525: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n526: \t\t\t}\n527: \n528: \t\t\t// a plugin that's enabled via MultiPoint can still be disabled for specific extension points\n529: \t\t\tif disabledSet.Has(ep.Name) {\n530: \t\t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Skipped disabled plugin for extension point\", \"plugin\", ep.Name, \"extension\", pluginType)\n531: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n532: \t\t\t}\n533: \n534: \t\t\t// if this plugin has already been enabled by the specific extension point,\n535: \t\t\t// the user intent is to override the default plugin or make some other explicit setting.\n536: \t\t\t// Either way, discard the MultiPoint value for this plugin.\n537: \t\t\t// This maintains expected behavior for overriding default plugins (see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99582)\n538: \t\t\tif enabledSet.has(ep.Name) {\n539: \t\t\t\toverridePlugins.insert(ep.Name)\n540: \t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"MultiPoint plugin is explicitly re-configured; overriding\", \"plugin\", ep.Name)\n541: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n542: \t\t\t}\n543: \n544: \t\t\t// if this plugin is already registered via MultiPoint, then this is\n545: \t\t\t// a double registration and an error in the config.\n546: \t\t\tif multiPointEnabled.has(ep.Name) {\n547: \t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugin %q already registered as %q\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\n548: \t\t\t}\n549: \n550: \t\t\t// we only need to update the multipoint set, since we already have the specific extension set from above\n551: \t\t\tmultiPointEnabled.insert(ep.Name)\n552: \t\t}\n553: \n554: \t\t// Reorder plugins. Here is the expected order:\n555: \t\t// - part 1: overridePlugins. Their order stay intact as how they're specified in regular extension point.\n556: \t\t// - part 2: multiPointEnabled - i.e., plugin defined in multipoint but not in regular extension point.\n557: \t\t// - part 3: other plugins (excluded by part 1 & 2) in regular extension point.\n558: \t\tnewPlugins := reflect.New(reflect.TypeOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()).Elem()\n559: \t\t// part 1\n560: \t\tfor _, name := range slice.CopyStrings(enabledSet.list) {\n561: \t\t\tif overridePlugins.has(name) {\n562: \t\t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n563: \t\t\t\tenabledSet.delete(name)\n564: \t\t\t}\n565: \t\t}\n566: \t\t// part 2\n567: \t\tfor _, name := range multiPointEnabled.list {\n568: \t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n569: \t\t}\n570: \t\t// part 3\n571: \t\tfor _, name := range enabledSet.list {\n572: \t\t\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\n573: \t\t}\n574: \t\tplugins.Set(newPlugins)\n575: \t}\n576: \treturn nil\n577: }\n578: \n579: func shouldHaveEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) bool {\n580: \tswitch p.(type) {\n581: \t// Only PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit plugins can (should) have EnqueueExtensions.\n582: \t// See the comment of EnqueueExtensions for more detailed reason here.\n583: \tcase framework.PreEnqueuePlugin, framework.PreFilterPlugin, framework.FilterPlugin, framework.ReservePlugin, framework.PermitPlugin:\n584: \t\treturn true\n585: \t}\n586: \treturn false\n587: }\n588: \n589: func (f *frameworkImpl) fillEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) {\n590: \tif !shouldHaveEnqueueExtensions(p) {\n591: \t\t// Ignore EnqueueExtensions from plugin which isn't PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit.\n592: \t\treturn\n593: \t}\n594: \n595: \text, ok := p.(framework.EnqueueExtensions)\n596: \tif !ok {\n597: \t\t// If interface EnqueueExtensions is not implemented, register the default enqueue extensions\n598: \t\t// to the plugin because we don't know which events the plugin is interested in.\n599: \t\t// This is to ensure backward compatibility.\n600: \t\tf.enqueueExtensions = append(f.enqueueExtensions, &defaultEnqueueExtension{pluginName: p.Name()})\n601: \t\treturn\n602: \t}\n603: \n604: \tf.enqueueExtensions = append(f.enqueueExtensions, ext)\n605: }\n606: \n607: // defaultEnqueueExtension is used when a plugin does not implement EnqueueExtensions interface.\n608: type defaultEnqueueExtension struct {\n609: \tpluginName string\n610: }\n611: \n612: func (p *defaultEnqueueExtension) Name() string { return p.pluginName }\n613: func (p *defaultEnqueueExtension) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n614: \t// need to return all specific cluster events with framework.All action instead of wildcard event\n615: \t// because the returning values are used to register event handlers.\n616: \t// If we return the wildcard here, it won't affect the event handlers registered by the plugin\n617: \t// and some events may not be registered in the event handlers.\n618: \treturn framework.UnrollWildCardResource()\n619: }\n620: \n621: func updatePluginList(pluginList interface{}, pluginSet config.PluginSet, pluginsMap map[string]framework.Plugin) error {\n622: \tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(pluginList).Elem()\n623: \tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\n624: \tset := sets.New[string]()\n625: \tfor _, ep := range pluginSet.Enabled {\n626: \t\tpg, ok := {\n632: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugin %q does not extend %s plugin\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\n633: \t\t}\n634: \n635: \t\tif set.Has(ep.Name) {\n636: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugin %q already registered as %q\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\n637: \t\t}\n638: \n639: \t\tset.Insert(ep.Name)\n640: \n641: \t\tnewPlugins := reflect.Append(plugins, reflect.ValueOf(pg))\n642: QueueSortFunc() framework.LessFunc {\n659: \tif f == nil {\n660: \t\t// If frameworkImpl is nil, simply keep their order unchanged.\n661: \t\t// NOTE: this is primarily for tests.\n662: \t\treturn It returns\n674: // *Status and its code is set to non-success if any of the plugins returns\n675: // anything but Success/Skip.\n676: // When it returns Skip status, returned PreFilterResult and other fields in status are just ignored,\n677: // and coupled Filter plugin/PreFilterExtensions() will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\n678: // If a non-success status is returned, then the scheduling cycle is aborted.\n679: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPreFilterPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (_ *framework.PreFilterResult, status *framework.Status) {\n680: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n681: \tskipPlugins := sets.New[string]()\n682: \tdefer func() {\n683: \t\tstate.SkipFilterPlugins = skipPlugins\n684: \t\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PreFilter, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n685: \t}()\n686: \tvar result *framework.PreFilterResult\n687: \tvar pluginsWithNodes []string\n688: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n689: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n690: \tif verboseLogs {\n691: \t\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"PreFilter\")\n692: \t}\n693: \tvar returnStatus *framework.Status\n694: \tfor _, pl := range f.preFilterPlugins {\n695: \t\tctx := ctx\n696: \t\tif verboseLogs {\n697: \t\t\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithName(logger, pl.Name())\n698: \t\t\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n699: \t\t}\n700: \t\tr, s := f.runPreFilterPlugin(ctx, pl, state, pod)\n701: \t\tif s.IsSkip() {\n702: \t\t\tskipPlugins.Insert(pl.Name())\n703: \t\t\tcontinue\n704: \t\t}\n705: \t\tif !s.IsSuccess() {\n706: \t\t\ts.SetPlugin(pl.Name())\n707: \t\t\tif s.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\n708: \t\t\t\t// In this case, the preemption shouldn't happen in this scheduling cycle.\n709: \t\t\t\t// So, no need to execute all PreFilter.\n710: \t\t\t\treturn nil, s\n711: \t\t\t}\n712: \t\t\tif s.Code() == framework.Unschedulable {\n713: \t\t\t\t// In this case, the preemption should happen later in this scheduling cycle.\n714: \t\t\t\t// So we need to execute all PreFilter.\n715: \t\t\t\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/119770\n716: \t\t\t\treturnStatus = s\n717: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n718: \t\t\t}\n719: \t\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"running PreFilter plugin %q: %w\", pl.Name(), s.AsError())).WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n720: \t\t}\n721: \t\tif !r.AllNodes() {\n722: \t\t\tpluginsWithNodes = append(pluginsWithNodes, pl.Name())\n723: \t\t}\n724: \t\tresult = result.Merge(r)\n725: \t\tif !result.AllNodes() && len(result.NodeNames) == 0 {\n726: \t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"node(s) didn't satisfy plugin(s) %v simultaneously\", pluginsWithNodes)\n727: \t\t\tif len(pluginsWithNodes) == 1 {\n728: \t\t\t\tmsg = fmt.Sprintf(\"node(s) didn't satisfy plugin %v\", pluginsWithNodes[0])\n729: \t\t\t}\n730: \n731: \t\t\t// When PreFilterResult filters out Nodes, the framework considers Nodes that are filtered out as getting \"UnschedulableAndUnresolvable\".\n732: \t\t\treturn result, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, msg)\n733: \t\t}\n734: \t}\n735: \treturn result, returnStatus\n736: }\n737: \n738: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPreFilterPlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PreFilterPlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n739: \tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\n740: \t\treturn pl.PreFilter(ctx, state, pod)\n741: \t}\n742: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n743: \tresult, status := pl.PreFilter(ctx, state, pod)\n744: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PreFilter, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n745: \treturn result, status\n746: }\n747: \n748: // execute all plugins.\n889: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPostFilterPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (_ *framework.PostFilterResult, status *framework.Status) {\n890: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n891: \tdefer func() {\n892: \t\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PostFilter, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n893: \t}()\n894: \n895: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n896: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n897: \tif \t\t\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithName(logger, pl.Name())\n909: \t\t\tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n910: \t\t}\n911: \t\tr, s := f.runPostFilterPlugin(ctx, pl, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n912: \t\tif s.IsSuccess() {\n913: \t\t\treturn r, s\n914: \t\t} else if s.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\n915: \t\t\treturn r, s.WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n916: \t\t} else if !s.IsRejected() {\n917: \t\t\t// Any status other than Success, Unschedulable or UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is Error.\n918: \t\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(s.AsError()).WithPlugin(pl.Name())\n919: \t\t} else if r != nil && r.Mode() != framework.ModeNoop {\n920: \t\t\tresult = r\n921: \t\t}\n922: \n923: \t\treasons = append(reasons, s.Reasons()...)\n924: \t\t// Record the first failed plugin unless we proved that\n925: \t\t// the latter is more relevant.\n926: \t\tif len(rejectorPlugin) == 0 {\n927: \t\t\trejectorPlugin = pl.Name()\n928: \t\t}\n929: \t}\n930: \n931: \treturn result, framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, reasons...).WithPlugin(rejectorPlugin)\n932: }\n933: \n934: func (f *frameworkImpl) runPostFilterPlugin(ctx context.Context, pl framework.PostFilterPlugin, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap framework.NodeToStatusMap) (*framework.PostFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n935: \tif !state.ShouldRecordPluginMetrics() {\n936: \t\treturn pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n937: \t}\n938: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n939: \tr, s := pl.PostFilter(ctx, state, pod, filteredNodeStatusMap)\n940: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PostFilter, pl.Name(), s.Code().String(), metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n941: \treturn r, s\n942: }\n943: \n944: // RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods runs the set of configured filter plugins\n945: // for nominated pod on the given node.\n946: // This function is called from two different places: Schedule and Preempt.\n947: // When it is called from Schedule, we want to test whether the pod is\n948: // schedulable on the node with all the existing pods on the node plus higher\n949: // and equal priority pods nominated to run on the node.\n950: // When it is called from Preempt, we should remove the victims of preemption\n951: // and add the nominated pods. Removal of the victims is done by\n952: // SelectVictimsOnNode(). Preempt removes victims from PreFilter state and\n953: // NodeInfo before calling this function.\n954: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, info *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n955: \tvar status *framework.Status\n956: \n957: \tpodsAdded := false\n958: \t// We run filters twice in some cases. If the node has greater or equal priority\n959: \t// nominated pods, we run them when those pods are added to PreFilter state and nodeInfo.\n960: \t// If all filters succeed in this pass, we run them again when these\n961: \t// nominated pods are not added. This second pass is necessary because some\n962: \t// filters such as inter-pod affinity may not pass without the nominated pods.\n963: \t// If there are no nominated pods for the node or if the first run of the\n964: \t// filters fail, we don't run the second pass.\n965: \t// We consider only equal or higher priority pods in the first pass, because\n966: \t// those are the current \"pod\" must yield to them and not take a space opened\n967: \t// for running them. It is ok if the current \"pod\" take resources freed for\n968: \t// lower priority pods.\n969: \t// Requiring that the new pod is schedulable in both circumstances ensures that\n970: \t// we are making a conservative decision: filters like resources and inter-pod\n971: \t// anti-affinity are more likely to fail when the nominated pods are treated\n972: \t// as running, while filters like pod affinity are more likely to fail when\n973: \t// the nominated pods are treated as not running. We can't just assume the\n974: \t// nominated pods are running because they are not running right now and in fact,\n975: \t// they may end up getting scheduled to a different node.\n976: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n977: \tlogger = klog.LoggerWithName(logger, \"FilterWithNominatedPods\")\n978: \tctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)\n979: \tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\n980: \t\tstateToUse := state\n981: \t\tnodeInfoToUse := info\n982: \t\tif i == 0 {\n983: \t\t\tvar err error\n984: \t\t\tpodsAdded, stateToUse, nodeInfoToUse, err = addNominatedPods(ctx, f, pod, state, info)\n985: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n986: \t\t\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n987: \t\t\t}\n988: \t\t} else if !podsAdded || !status.IsSuccess() {\n989: \t\t\tbreak\n990: \t\t}\n991: \n992: \t\tstatus = f.RunFilterPlugins(ctx, stateToUse, pod, nodeInfoToUse)\n993: \t\tif !status.IsSuccess() && !status.IsRejected() {\n994: \t\t\treturn status\n995: \t\t}\n996: \t}\n997: \n998: \treturn status\n999: }\n1000: \n1001: // addNominatedPods adds pods with equal or greater priority which are nominated\n1002: // to run on the node. It returns 1) whether any pod was added, 2) augmented cycleState,\n1003: // 3) augmented nodeInfo.\n1004: func addNominatedPods(ctx context.Context, fh framework.Handle, pod *v1.Pod, state *framework.CycleState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) (bool, *framework.CycleState, *framework.NodeInfo, error) {\n1005: \tif fh == nil {\n1006: \t\t// This may happen only in tests.\n1007: \t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\n1008: \t}\n1009: \tnominatedPodInfos := fh.NominatedPodsForNode(nodeInfo.Node().Name)\n1010: \tif len(nominatedPodInfos) == 0 {\n1011: \t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, nil\n1012: \t}\n1013: \tnodeInfoOut := nodeInfo.Snapshot()\n1014: \tstateOut := state.Clone()\n1015: \tpodsAdded := false\n1016: \tfor _, pi := range nominatedPodInfos {\n1017: \t\tif corev1.PodPriority(pi.Pod) >= corev1.PodPriority(pod) && pi.Pod.UID != pod.UID {\n1018: \t\t\tnodeInfoOut.AddPodInfo(pi)\n1019: \t\t\tstatus := fh.RunPreFilterExtensionAddPod(ctx, stateOut, pod, pi, nodeInfoOut)\n1020: \t\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n1021: \t\t\t\treturn false, state, nodeInfo, status.AsError()\n1022: \t\t\t}\n1023: \t\t\tpodsAdded = true\n1024: \t\t}\n1025: \t}\n1026: \treturn podsAdded, stateOut, nodeInfoOut, nil\n1027: }\n1028: \n1029: // RunPreScorePlugins runs the set of configured pre-score plugins. If any\n1030: // of these plugins returns any status other than Success/Skip, the given pod is rejected.\n1031: // When it returns Skip status, other fields in status are just ignored,\n1032: // and coupled Score plugin will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\n1033: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPreScorePlugins(\n1034: \tctx context.Context,\n1035: \tstate *framework.CycleState,\n1036: \tpod *v1.Pod,\n1037: \tnodes []*framework.NodeInfo,\n1038: ) (status *framework.Status) {\n1039: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n1040: \tskipPlugins := sets.New[string]()\n1041: \tdefer func() {\n1042: \t\tstate.SkipScorePlugins = skipPlugins\n1043: \t\tmetrics.FrameworkExtensionPointDuration.WithLabelValues(metrics.PreScore, status.Code().String(), f.profileName).Observe(metrics.SinceInSeconds(startTime))\n1044: \t}()\n1045: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n1046: \tverboseLogs := logger.V(4).Enabled()\n1047: \tif verboseLogs {\n1048: \t}\n1072: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n1073: \tstatus := pl.PreScore(ctx, state, pod, nodes)\n1074: \tf.metricsRecorder.ObservePluginDurationAsync(metrics.PreScore, pl.Name(), status.Code().String(), Otherwise, if any of the\n1421: // plugins returns \"Wait\", then this function will create and add waiting pod\n1422: // to a map of currently waiting pods and return status with \"Wait\" code.\n1423: // Pod will remain waiting pod for the minimum duration returned by the permit plugins.\n1424: func (f *frameworkImpl) RunPermitPlugins(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod.Name)\n1467: \t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"One or more plugins asked to wait and no plugin rejected pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n1468: \t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Wait, msg)\n1469: This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n65: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n66: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n67: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n68: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n69: \\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\\n70: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n71: \\n72: \\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n73: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\\n74: \\t// will be used.\\n75: \\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\\n76: \\n77: \\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n78: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\\n79: \\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\\n80: \\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\\n81: \\n82: \\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\\n85: \\t// with the \\\"default-scheduler\\\" profile, if present here.\\n86: \\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\\n87: \\n88: \\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\\n89: \\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\\n90: \\tExtenders []Extender\\n91: \\n92: \\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\\n93: \\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\\n94: \\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\\n95: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n96: \\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\\n100: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\\n101: \\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\\n102: \\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \\\"spec.schedulerName\\\", then the pod\\n103: \\t// is scheduled with this profile.\\n104: \\tSchedulerName string\\n105: \\n106: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n107: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n108: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n109: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n110: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n111: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n112: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n113: \\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\\n114: \\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\\n115: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n116: \\n117: \\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\\n118: \\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\\n119: \\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\\n120: \\t// should be disabled.\\n121: \\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\\n122: \\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\\n123: \\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\\n124: \\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\\n125: \\tPlugins *Plugins\\n126: \\n127: \\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\\n128: \\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\\n129: \\t// for that plugin.\\n130: \\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\\n131: }\\n132: \\n133: // Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\\n134: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\\n135: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\\n136: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\\n137: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\\n138: type Plugins struct {\\n139: \\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\\n140: \\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\\n141: \\n142: \\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\\n143: \\tQueueSort PluginSet\\n144: \\n145: \\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"PreFilter\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n146: \\tPreFilter PluginSet\\n147: \\n148: \\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\\n149: \\tFilter PluginSet\\n150: \\n151: \\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\\n152: \\tPostFilter PluginSet\\n153: \\n154: \\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\\n155: \\tPreScore PluginSet\\n156: \\n157: \\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\\n158: \\tScore PluginSet\\n159: \\n160: \\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\\n161: \\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\\n162: \\tReserve PluginSet\\n163: \\n164: \\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\\n165: \\tPermit PluginSet\\n166: \\n167: \\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\\n168: \\tPreBind PluginSet\\n169: \\n170: \\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"Bind\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n171: \\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\\n172: \\tBind PluginSet\\n173: \\n174: \\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\\n175: \\tPostBind PluginSet\\n176: \\n177: \\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\\n178: \\tMultiPoint PluginSet\\n179: }\\n180: \\n181: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\\n182: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\\n183: type PluginSet struct {\\n184: \\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\\n185: \\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\\n186: \\tEnabled []Plugin\\n187: \\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\\n188: \\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \\\"*\\\" should be provided.\\n189: \\tDisabled []Plugin\\n190: }\\n191: \\n192: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\\n193: type Plugin struct {\\n194: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin\\n195: \\tName string\\n196: \\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\\n197: \\tWeight int32\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\\n201: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n202: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\\n203: type PluginConfig struct {\\n204: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\\n205: \\tName string\\n206: \\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n207: \\tArgs runtime.Object\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: /*\\n211:  * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\\n212:  */\\n213: const (\\n214: \\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\\n215: \\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\\n216: \\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\\n217: \\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\\n218: \\n219: \\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\\n220: \\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\\n221: \\n222: \\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\\n223: \\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\\n224: \\n225: \\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\\n226: \\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\\n227: )\\n228: \\n229: // Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\\n230: func (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\\n231: \\tif p == nil {\\n232: \\t\\treturn nil\\n233: \\t}\\n234: \\textensions := []PluginSet{\\n235: \\t\\tp.PreEnqueue,\\n236: \\t\\tp.PreFilter,\\n237: \\t\\tp.Filter,\\n238: \\t\\tp.PostFilter,\\n239: \\t\\tp.Reserve,\\n240: \\t\\tp.PreScore,\\n241: \\t\\tp.Score,\\n242: \\t\\tp.PreBind,\\n243: \\t\\tp.Bind,\\n244: \\t\\tp.PostBind,\\n245: \\t\\tp.Permit,\\n246: \\t\\tp.QueueSort,\\n247: \\t}\\n248: \\tn := sets.New[string]()\\n249: \\tfor _, e := range extensions {\\n250: \\t\\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\\n251: \\t\\t\\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\\n252: \\t\\t}\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn sets.List(n)\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\\n258: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\\n259: type Extender struct {\\n260: \\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\\n261: \\tURLPrefix string\\n262: \\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\\n263: \\tFilterVerb string\\n264: \\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\\n265: \\tPreemptVerb string\\n266: \\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\\n267: \\tPrioritizeVerb string\\n268: \\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\\n269: \\t// The weight should be a positive integer\\n270: \\tWeight int64\\n271: \\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\\n272: \\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\\n273: \\t// can implement this function.\\n274: \\tBindVerb string\\n275: \\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\\n276: \\tEnableHTTPS bool\\n277: \\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\\n278: \\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\\n279: \\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender.
